channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel slams Palestinian 'deception scheme' over claim it halted terror rewards program
item.description: Reports claiming the Palestinian Authority canceled its "pay for slay" terror rewards program have been questioned by critics. An Israeli spokesman called the move a "deception scheme.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of US organizations condemn Trump Gaza plan
item.description: Social justice organizations based in the U.S. issued a joint statement Monday condemning President Trump’s plans to redevelop the Gaza Strip, effectively displacing Palestinians from their homeland.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: With metal detectors and patience, amateur treasure hunters unearth pieces of British history
item.description: Malcolm Weale was in a farmer's field with his metal detector when he unearthed a tiny and dirt-covered object.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian cybercrime network targeted for sanctions across US, UK and Australia
item.description: The U.S., U.K. and Australia have sanctioned a Russian web-hosting services provider and two Russian men who administer the service in support of Russian ransomware syndicate LockBit.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump pauses enforcement of foreign bribery ban
item.description: President Trump has signed an executive order to direct the Department of Justice to stop enforcing a law that prevents bribery toward foreign government officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel’s fatal shooting of a pregnant Palestinian woman puts the focus on West Bank violence
item.description: KAFR AL-LABAD, West Bank (AP) — The call came in the middle of the night, Mohammed Shula said. His daughter-in-law, eight months pregnant with her first child, was whispering. There was panic in her voice. “Help, please,” Shula recalled her saying. “You have to save us.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO allies await US defense secretary's visit, the first by a member of the Trump administration
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will on Wednesday become the first member of the new Trump administration to visit NATO, where the allies are keen to understand how America now plans to influence the course of the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas brushes off Trump and insists all parties must be committed to ceasefire
item.description: Hamas has brushed off President Donald Trump’s threat that “all hell” will break out if it does not release the remaining Israeli hostages held in the Gaza Strip by Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Corruption survey gives many nations worst scores in over a decade. South Sudan slides to the bottom
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Many countries had their worst showing in more than a decade in an index released Tuesday that serves as a barometer of public sector corruption worldwide, from leading powers such as the United States and France to authoritarian nations such as Russia and Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Haitian migrants share harrowing stories of abuse as Dominican Republic ramps up deportations
item.description: Dominican officials have been deporting at least 10,000 Haitian immigrants a week under a harsh new policy widely criticized by civil organizations.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: UN suspends its humanitarian work in Houthi rebels' stronghold in Yemen after more staff detentions
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it suspended its humanitarian operations in the stronghold of Yemen’s Houthi rebels after they detained eight more U.N. staffers, affecting the global response to one of the world’s worst humanitarian disasters. In a statement, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What Mexico's richest man thinks Trump should do in his second term
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Carlos Slim, Mexico’s richest man and one of the world's wealthiest, addressed a range of topics at his annual conference on Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says ceasefire deal should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all hostages by Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that a precarious ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all the remaining hostages it is holding in Gaza by midday on Saturday — though he also said that such a decision would be up to Israel.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and SAM MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he has talked to Xi since inauguration
item.description: President Trump said he has spoken to Chinese President Xi Jinping since his swearing in last month, suggesting they have spoken in the days since he imposed tariffs on Beijing. “I happen to like him a lot, President Xi,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trump signs off on new steel and aluminum tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed proclamations to bolster tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the United States and crack down on attempts by China and Russia to evade penalties. Trump in 2018 announced 25 percent tariffs on steel and 10 percent tariffs on aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Hamas postpones upcoming hostage release, risking ceasefire collapse
item.description: Hamas announced Monday it will hold back releasing hostages at the end of the week until further notice, according to a statement by the group reported by multiple media outlets. Hamas, designated as a terrorist organization by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: French girl, 11, found dead near school, murder investigation underway
item.description: Investigators in France have launched a murder investigation after a missing 11-year-old girl was found dead in a wooded area near her school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With Trump in the White House, Iranians mark the anniversary of the 1979 Islamic Revolution
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Tens of thousands of Iranians marked the anniversary of the country's 1979 Islamic Revolution, the first such rally since President Donald Trump returned to the White House and restarted his “maximum pressure” campaign targeting Tehran.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian protesters block key bridge, roads to mark 100 days since deadly canopy collapse
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Striking university students in Serbia blocked a bridge in Belgrade as well as roads throughout the Balkan country on Sunday to mark 100 days since the collapse of a concrete canopy at a train station which killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marine identified as victim of surveillance flight that crashed in Philippines
item.description: Military officials identified a U.S. Marine who died in a plane crash in the Philippines last week as Sgt. Jacob M. Durham, a California native.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kosovo's governing party wins parliamentary election but without majority, preliminary results show
item.description: PRISTINA, Kosovo (AP) — Preliminary results showed that Prime Minister Albin Kurti ’s party won Kosovo's parliamentary election Sunday but without the majority needed to govern alone, as talks on normalizing ties with rival Serbia remain stalled and foreign funding for one of Europe's poorest countr
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US officials are bound for Europe for top-level talks on Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump’s national security adviser said Sunday that top administration officials will meet with European officials this week about how to end the war in Ukraine, nearly three years after Russia launched an all-out invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Parents of American murdered by Hamas make 'plea' to Trump after latest hostage release
item.description: The parents of murdered Israeli-American hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin pleaded with President Donald Trump to get the remaining 76 hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: They helped US order airstrikes against Taliban. Now Trump's moves have left those Afghans in limbo
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — They helped the U.S. military order airstrikes against Taliban and Islamic State fighters and worked as drivers and translators during America's longest war. They were set to start new lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Days before Trump halted funding, an ex-Israeli hostage was held at UNRWA school in Gaza, she reveals
item.description: Former Israeli hostage Emily Damari said she was held in an UNRWA center in Gaza. Damari was recently released as part of the hostages for ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defaced Holocaust mural finds new home in Rome's Shoah Museum
item.description: Contemporary pop artist aleXsandro Palombo is speaking out after his murals showing Holocaust survivors were defaced multiple times in Italy in apparent acts of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage released 491 days after he was kidnapped and his family was slaughtered
item.description: Eli Sharabi, who was released after spending 491 days in Hamas captivity, will return home without his wife or two daughters, who were killed on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil's socialist president tells citizens not to buy expensive grocery items as food prices rise
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said in a video message posted to social media that citizens should not buy expensive food items to deal with rising food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI, scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
item.description: Hundreds of papyrus scrolls were found in the 1750s in the remains of a lavish villa at the Roman town of Herculaneum.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Saudis could 'de-radicalize' Palestinians: Trump envoy
item.description: “We’ll have a lot of clarity for you in the coming days,” a Trump envoy says of questions about Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
